# Bricks — Full Technical Reference

> Version: 0.4.36
> PyPI: bricks-ai (core), bricks-ai-stdlib (stdlib)
> Import: `from bricks import Bricks`

---

## Architecture

```
Natural language task
        │
        ▼
  BlueprintComposer          ← one LLM call (cached after first run)
        │  writes YAML
        ▼
  BlueprintDefinition        ← validated Pydantic model
        │
        ▼
  BlueprintValidator         ← dry-run: checks types, connections, missing inputs
        │
        ▼
  BlueprintEngine.run()      ← deterministic Python execution, step by step
        │
        ▼
  ExecutionContext            ← tracks outputs, resolves ${variable} references
```

No LLM is involved after blueprint composition. Execution is pure Python.

---

## Public API

### Import

```python
from bricks import Bricks
```

### `Bricks.default()`

Zero-config entry point. Reads API key from environment.

```python
engine = Bricks.default(
    model="claude-haiku-4-5",   # LiteLLM model string; overrides BRICKS_MODEL env var
    api_key="",                  # explicit key; empty = read from env
    provider=None,               # custom LLMProvider instance; overrides model/api_key
)
```

Default model: `claude-haiku-4-5` (overridden by `BRICKS_MODEL` env var).

### `Bricks.from_config()`

Boot from a structured YAML config file. No LLM call at boot time.

```python
engine = Bricks.from_config(
    config_path="examples/agent.yaml",   # str or Path; .yaml or .yml
    api_key="",                           # overrides key in config file if provided
    registry=None,                        # BrickRegistry | None; defaults to all installed packs
)
```

### `Bricks.from_skill()`

Boot from a markdown skill description. Makes one LLM call to extract domain keywords.

```python
engine = Bricks.from_skill(
    skill_path="examples/skill.md",   # str or Path; must be .md
    api_key="",
    model="claude-haiku-4-5",         # model for the boot LLM call
    registry=None,
)
```

### `engine.execute()`

Execute a natural-language task end-to-end.

```python
result = engine.execute(
    task="filter active customers and calculate total revenue",
    inputs={"api_response": raw_data},   # dict for ${inputs.X} references; optional
)
```

**Returns:**

```python
{
    "outputs":      dict,   # final output values from the blueprint
    "cache_hit":    bool,   # True if blueprint served from store (zero tokens)
    "api_calls":    int,    # number of LLM calls made (0 on cache hit)
    "tokens_used":  int,    # total tokens consumed
}
```

---

## Brick authoring

### Function-based brick

```python
from bricks.core.brick import brick
from bricks.core.registry import BrickRegistry

@brick(tags=["data"], destructive=False)
def filter_active(records: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    return [r for r in records if r.get("status") == "active"]

registry = BrickRegistry()
registry.register("filter_active", filter_active, filter_active.__brick_meta__)
```

### Class-based brick

```python
from bricks.core.brick import BaseBrick, BrickModel
from bricks.core.models import BrickMeta
from typing import Any

class FilterActive(BaseBrick):
    class Meta:
        tags = ["data"]
        destructive = False

    class Input(BrickModel):
        records: list[dict]

    class Output(BrickModel):
        filtered: list[dict]

    def execute(self, inputs: BrickModel, metadata: BrickMeta) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {"filtered": [r for r in inputs.records if r.get("status") == "active"]}
```

### Teardown hooks

```python
def cleanup(inputs: dict, error: Exception) -> None:
    # called on failure — close connections, delete temp files, etc.
    ...

@brick(tags=["io"], destructive=True, teardown=cleanup)
def write_to_db(records: list[dict]) -> dict:
    ...
```

For class-based bricks, override `teardown(self, inputs, metadata, error)`.

The engine calls teardown on the failing step, then reverse-teardown all completed steps, then re-raises the original error.

---

## Blueprint YAML format

```yaml
name: crm-pipeline
description: Filter active customers and sum revenue
inputs:
  raw_json: str

steps:
  - name: parse
    brick: parse_json_string
    inputs:
      json_str: ${inputs.raw_json}

  - name: filter
    brick: filter_dict_list
    inputs:
      records: ${parse.result}
      key: status
      value: active

  - name: count
    brick: count_items
    inputs:
      items: ${filter.result}

  - name: total
    brick: sum_numbers
    inputs:
      numbers: ${filter.result[*].monthly_revenue}

outputs:
  active_count: ${count.result}
  total_revenue: ${total.result}
```

**Reference syntax:** `${step_name.output_key}` — resolved by `ReferenceResolver` at runtime.

---

## Configuration (agent.yaml)

```yaml
name: my-agent
model: claude-haiku-4-5        # any LiteLLM model string

store:
  enabled: true                # cache blueprints (recommended)
  backend: file                # "file" (persistent) or "memory" (session-only)
  path: blueprints/
  ttl_days: 30

selector:
  top_k: 20                    # bricks included in compose prompt (5–50)

skills:
  - skills/crm.md              # markdown skill description files
```

See [examples/agent.yaml](examples/agent.yaml) for the full annotated reference.

---

## Standard library (bricks-ai-stdlib)

100+ pre-tested bricks across 7 modules:

| Module | Example bricks |
|--------|---------------|
| `data_transformation` | `parse_json_string`, `filter_dict_list`, `group_by_key`, `flatten_list` |
| `string_processing` | `split_string`, `join_strings`, `regex_extract`, `truncate_string` |
| `math_numeric` | `sum_numbers`, `average_numbers`, `round_number`, `calculate_aggregates` |
| `list_operations` | `count_items`, `sort_list`, `deduplicate_list`, `slice_list` |
| `validation` | `validate_email`, `validate_url`, `assert_not_empty`, `coerce_types` |
| `encoding_security` | `base64_encode`, `base64_decode`, `hash_string`, `mask_sensitive` |
| `date_time` | `parse_datetime`, `format_datetime`, `convert_timezone`, `date_diff` |

---

## MCP server

Bricks exposes a `execute_task` MCP tool. Any MCP-compatible host (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) can call it.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "bricks": {
      "command": "bricks",
      "args": ["serve"],
      "env": { "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY": "sk-ant-..." }
    }
  }
}
```

Config file location:
- macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
- Windows: `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`

---

## Community packs

Any Python package named `bricks-{name}` with a `bricks.packs` entry point auto-registers on `Bricks.default()`:

```toml
# pyproject.toml
[project.entry-points."bricks.packs"]
mypack = "bricks_mypack"
```

```python
# bricks_mypack/__init__.py
from bricks.core.brick import brick
from bricks.core.registry import BrickRegistry

@brick(tags=["custom"])
def my_brick(value: str) -> str:
    return value.upper()

def register(registry: BrickRegistry) -> None:
    registry.register("my_brick", my_brick, my_brick.__brick_meta__)
```

---

## Benchmark results (v0.4.31, CRM-pipeline scenario)

| | BricksEngine | Raw LLM |
|---|---|---|
| Correctness | 100% (all models, all seeds) | 0–60% |
| 10-run consistency | 10/10 | 6/10 |
| 20-run token cost | 3,327 tokens total | 75,880 tokens |

Models tested: Claude Haiku, Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o-mini.

---

## Key source files

- [packages/core/src/bricks/api.py](packages/core/src/bricks/api.py) — public API (`Bricks` class)
- [packages/core/src/bricks/core/engine.py](packages/core/src/bricks/core/engine.py) — `BlueprintEngine`
- [packages/core/src/bricks/core/brick.py](packages/core/src/bricks/core/brick.py) — `@brick`, `BaseBrick`, `BrickModel`
- [packages/core/src/bricks/core/registry.py](packages/core/src/bricks/core/registry.py) — `BrickRegistry`
- [packages/core/src/bricks/core/models.py](packages/core/src/bricks/core/models.py) — `BrickMeta`, `BlueprintDefinition`, `StepDefinition`
- [packages/core/src/bricks/core/validation.py](packages/core/src/bricks/core/validation.py) — `BlueprintValidator`
- [packages/core/src/bricks/orchestrator/runtime.py](packages/core/src/bricks/orchestrator/runtime.py) — `RuntimeOrchestrator`
- [packages/core/src/bricks/ai/composer.py](packages/core/src/bricks/ai/composer.py) — `BlueprintComposer`
- [packages/stdlib/src/bricks_stdlib/](packages/stdlib/src/bricks_stdlib/) — standard library bricks
- [examples/agent.yaml](examples/agent.yaml) — full config reference
- [examples/skill.md](examples/skill.md) — skill description format
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) — version history
